description_long: This is an irregularly shaped, medium-sized rock with a rugged, angular profile and a predominantly gray coloration. The surface is coarse and uneven, featuring jagged edges, small crevices, and patches of brownish and yellowish mineral deposits. The rock appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or signs of artificial modification. Its form is elongated and tapers at one end, suggesting it may have fractured naturally. The texture is rough and weathered, indicating exposure to the elements over time. This rock would blend naturally into urban parks, roadside verges, or landscaped areas as a natural obstacle or decorative element.
description: A rugged, gray, and angular rock with jagged edges, rough texture, and patches of brown and yellow mineral deposits.
description_view_0: Tall, upright view showing the rock's vertical, jagged face and uneven base.
description_view_1: Side profile revealing the elongated, flat, and tapered shape with visible stratification and rough surface.
description_view_2: Narrow end-on view highlighting the compact width and irregular, blocky end.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile displaying more of the rock's rough, weathered surface and mineral coloration.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped roadside verge
